title: Archer (ACHR) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Archer reported $1.8 billion in liquidity with less than $100 million in debt, positioning the company as having one of the strongest positions in the sector. Management projected Q2 adjusted EBITDA losses between $170-$200 million, reflecting expanded investments across civil, defense, and software initiatives. The company achieved a major milestone by becoming the first eVTOL manufacturer to complete Phase II of the FAA's four-phase type certification process. Management indicated they are deep in Phase I with maintenance of compliance completed, suggesting final administrative approvals remain on the horizon. Archer currently operates two aircraft with plans to build 8-10 in initial production. The company has established infrastructure capable of scaling to 50 aircraft annually once certification milestones are achieved. Revenue began growing following expanded operations at Hawthorne Airport in Los Angeles. A major strategic focus involves developing a clean sheet hybrid dual-use autonomous aircraft in partnership with Anderol, targeting substantial U.S. and international defense procurement opportunities. Management expressed confidence in winning phase down selects during 2026. Archer was selected as a partner in three successful EIPP applications across eight states and secured the role as official air taxi provider for LA '28 Olympic Games, with commercial operations expected to launch later in 2026.
